items.question: A company has an application with users in both Australia and Brazil. All the company infrastructure is currently provisioned in the Asia Pacific (Sydney) Region in Australia, and Brazilian users are experiencing high latency. What should the company do to reduce latency?

options.A: Implement AWS Direct Connect for users in Brazil
options.B: Provision resources in the South America (São Paulo) Region in Brazil
options.C: Use AWS Transit Gateway to quickly route users from Brazil to the application
options.D: Launch additional Amazon EC2 instances in Sydney to handle the demand

items.question: A company is based in the us-east-1 Region and has a satellite office in the eu-west-2 Region. The company wants to use Amazon WorkSpaces to host its internal web portal and virtual desktops for employees. What should the company do to minimize latency and ensure the best possible performance for employees?

options.A: Deploy the internal web portal and virtual desktops to us-east-1 only. Use an Amazon CloudFront distribution for the users in eu-west-2.
options.B: Deploy the internal web portal to us-east-1 only. Deploy the virtual desktops to us-east-1 and eu-west-2.
options.C: Deploy the internal web portal to us-east-1 and eu-west-2. Deploy the virtual desktops on network optimized Amazon EC2 instances to us-east-1 only.
options.D: Deploy the internal web portal and virtual desktops to us-east-1 and eu-west-2.

items.question: A company is building an application that needs to deliver images and videos globally with minimal latency. Which approach can the company use to accomplish this in a cost effective manner?

options.A: Deliver the content through Amazon CloudFront.
options.B: Store the content on Amazon S3 and enable S3 cross-region replication.
options.C: Implement a VPN across multiple AWS Regions.
options.D: Deliver the content through AWS PrivateLink.

items.question: A company is designing an application hosted in a single AWS Region serving end-users spread across the world. The company wants to provide the end-users low latency access to the application data. Which of the following services will help fulfill this requirement?

items.question: A company must ensure that its endpoint for a database instance remains the same after a single Availability Zone service interruption. The application needs to resume database operations without the need for manual administrative intervention. How can these requirements be met?

options.B: Configure Amazon RDS Multi-Availability Zone deployments with automatic failover to the standby.
options.C: Add multiple Application Load Balancers and deploy the database instance with AWS Elastic Beanstalk.
options.D: Deploy a single Network Load Balancer to distribute incoming traffic across multiple Amazon CloudFront origins.

options.B: Set up Amazon S3 cross-region replication to another AWS Region
options.C: Configure the AWS Backup service to back up to the data to another AWS Region
options.D: Take Amazon S3 bucket snapshots and copy that data to another AWS Region

items.question: How does the AWS global infrastructure offer high availability and fault tolerance to its users?

options.B: The AWS infrastructure consists of subnets containing various Availability Zones with multiple data centers located in the same geographic location.
options.C: AWS allows users to choose AWS Regions and data centers so that users can select the closest data centers in different Regions.
